fact: July 9 is exactly 190 days into the year, which means there are exactly 175 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around July 9 in North America - some species buzz so loud they can damage your hearing if you get too close!
Why do fireflies blink like crazy this time of year? July 9 falls right in the middle of firefly mating season when they're desperately trying to find love!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 9 is smack in the middle of winter - penguins are huddling together while kids up north are swimming!
Corn plants grow so fast around this date they literally make crackling sounds - farmers say you can actually hear corn growing on quiet July nights!
The sun rises before 6 AM and sets after 8 PM in most of the northern United States, giving kids almost 15 hours of potential outdoor adventure time!
July 9 falls smack between the summer solstice and the autumn equinox - it's like the exact middle of summer's party!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying around July 9 because warm, humid weather makes them breed like crazy - a single female can lay 300 eggs at once!
Ocean water temperatures peak around this time in the Northern Hemisphere, making it the absolute best time for swimming without turning into a human popsicle!
Sunflowers are tilting their giant heads to follow the sun all day long around July 9 - they're like nature's solar panels with attitude!
In northern Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around July 9 - kids there can play outside at midnight and it still looks like afternoon!
